name: Artichokes with Herbs
recipeInstructions:
1. With scissors or a sharp knife, cut 1 3/4 inches off the top of each artichoke; remove the stems.
2. Pull leaves open at the top, removing the center leaves and scraping out the choke with a spoon.
3. In a blender or food processor, combine carrot, onion, parsley, dried herbs (basil, oregano, dill weed), garlic, salt, and pepper to taste. Process until finely chopped.
4. Stuff the herb mixture between the leaves of the artichokes.
5. Place a cooking rack, white wine, and 1/2 cup water in a pressure cooker with a capacity of 4- or 6-quarts.
6. Put the artichokes on the rack; secure the cover tightly.
7. Place the pressure regulator on the vent pipe.
8. Cook for 20 minutes at 15 pounds pressure, allowing the pressure to regulate slowly.
9. Immediately remove the cooker from heat and release the pressure.
10. Remove the artichokes and rack; keep them warm.
11. Boil the liquid left in the pressure cooker for about 5 minutes or until reduced by half.
12. Stir in red pepper flakes.
13. To serve, spoon the cooking liquid over the artichokes.
Per serving: Approximately 115 calories; 2.5 grams protein; 11 grams carbohydrates; 3.5 grams fat (27% of calories from fat); 0 milligrams cholesterol; 67 milligrams sodium.
